headline: Latest News In E-Commerce - South Korea's Social Commerce Boom Driven By Innovation
summary: The South Korean social commerce market is experiencing robust growth, projected to reach USD 125.57 billion by 2026, marking a 12.1% annual increase. This expansion is driven by evolving consumer behaviors and the integration of live shopping formats and AI-driven platforms, which are enhancing engagement and personalization within the digital retail environment. Key platforms such as Naver, Kakao, and Coupang are pivotal in shaping this landscape, with trends focusing on live shopping, AI...

headline: The Records Got the Headlines. Oil Is the Real Story.
summary: The S&P 500 closed at a new all-time high on Wednesday. So did the Nasdaq. And Brent crude hit $101.73 a barrel, its highest level in years, after Iran seized two container ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Two of those three facts dominated the financial press. I'll let you guess which one didn't.

I'll let you guess which one didn't.

headline: Is Adobe’s AI Agent Push And US$25 Billion Buyback Altering The Investment Case For Adobe (ADBE)?
summary: In April 2026, Adobe unveiled a broad suite of agentic AI offerings across CX Enterprise, GenStudio, Firefly and Experience Manager, while its board authorized a new US$25.00 billion share repurchase program running through April 2030. By combining deep ecosystem partnerships, industry‑specific AI collaborations with firms like Omnicom, and a long-dated buyback, Adobe is signaling a push to anchor itself at the center of enterprise-grade, governed AI for marketing, commerce and creative...
